purpose: Define preflight evidence and boundaries for a future user-attended, read-only DevHub observation without opening DevHub or creating private browser artifacts.

synthetic_user_attended_preflight_eviden.assertion: The user will remain present for any authenticated DevHub observation and will complete all login, MFA, CAPTCHA, security, and consent prompts manually.
The worker may only observe user-visible read-only account surfaces and record redacted metadata.
No credentials, cookies, browser state, screenshots, traces, HAR files, raw authenticated HTML, downloaded documents, payment details, or local private paths may be stored.

manual_login_boundaries: The worker must not request, receive, type, paste, infer, save, replay, or transform credentials.
The user must manually select sign-in controls and complete wetten.overheid.nl authentication in the visible browser.
The user must manually complete MFA, CAPTCHA, security prompts, account recovery, consent prompts, and any identity checks.
The worker must not create accounts, change account settings, enroll MFA, recover passwords, or bypass access controls.
If the login flow requires a consequential choice outside read-only observation, the worker must stop and record a refused-action journal event without private values.
redaction_expectations: Replace names, addresses, permit numbers, application numbers, IVR numbers, phone numbers, emails, parcel identifiers, payment details, document names, and free-text comments with stable placeholders before persistence.
Store only category labels, boolean presence flags, count buckets, workflow stage labels, and source-neutral notes needed for guardrail review.
Do not persist raw authenticated page text, screenshots, traces, HAR files, downloaded files, cookies, local storage, session storage, or browser profiles.
Do not persist private local file paths or filenames from user uploads or attachment lists.
Do not copy visible account-scoped values into test fixtures; fixtures must stay synthetic.
abort_conditions: Any request for credentials, password recovery, MFA enrollment, CAPTCHA solving, or account creation appears.
The page asks for payment detail entry, payment submission, purchase confirmation, or receipt storage.
The next available action would submit, certify, upload, schedule, cancel, withdraw, reactivate, extend, purchase, or change an official record.
The user is no longer present or cannot confirm that observation should continue.
The worker cannot distinguish read-only observation from a consequential state transition.
A browser or tool attempts to save auth state, screenshots, traces, HAR files, raw authenticated HTML, downloads, or private page values.
Unexpected third-party, unsupported, or non-allowlisted authenticated surfaces appear.

post_observation_review_placeholders.review_prompt: Confirm the observation journal contains only redacted metadata and no account-scoped raw values.
Confirm each observed surface remained read-only and did not require consequential action gating.
Confirm any encountered abort condition stopped the workflow before private data capture or official action.
Identify public PP&D source pages needed to interpret observed labels without relying on private DevHub values.

prohibited_artifacts.reason: Would preserve private authenticated session material.
Would preserve cookies, local storage, or session storage.
Would preserve private browsing profile data.
Credentials must remain unknown to the worker.
Authenticated downloads may contain private case data.
Network archives may contain private URLs, tokens, and payloads.
Local user paths can expose private identity and project details.
Raw page content can contain private account-scoped values.
Screenshots can capture private account data.
Session recordings can capture account-scoped DevHub behavior and values.
Browser storage state can preserve cookies and authenticated local storage.
Traces can capture DOM, network, and input details.
